title: ActiveCampaign vs Campaign Monitor (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ActiveCampaign vs Campaign Monitor. See pricing, features, and which email marketing tool suits your business needs.
h1: ActiveCampaign vs Campaign Monitor: Detailed Comparison
intro: Both ActiveCampaign and Campaign Monitor are established email marketing platforms, but they serve different business needs. ActiveCampaign emphasizes automation and CRM capabilities, while Campaign Monitor focuses on design-first templates and multi-brand management. This comparison will help you determine which platform aligns best with your marketing goals and team structure.
choose_a_if: You need advanced automation workflows and lead scoring capabilities
CRM functionality integrated with your email marketing is essential
You want SMS marketing, site tracking, and dynamic content personalization
You prioritize customer reviews and satisfaction (higher Trustpilot rating)
choose_b_if: You're an agency managing multiple client brands
Budget is a priority and you want the lowest starting price
Beautiful, drag-and-drop email templates are your main concern
You need strong multi-brand management and transactional email features

faq.q: Is ActiveCampaign better than Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: It depends on your needs. ActiveCampaign excels in automation, CRM, and advanced features with higher customer satisfaction ratings (4.5 G2, 4.1 Trustpilot). Campaign Monitor is better if you're an agency needing multi-brand management and prefer a design-focused, user-friendly interface. Choose based on whether you need robust automation or elegant simplicity.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ActiveCampaign or Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: Campaign Monitor starts at $11/month, making it $4 cheaper than ActiveCampaign's $15/month entry point. However, pricing scales with subscriber count, so the final cost depends on your list size and feature requirements. Request quotes for your specific needs to compare total cost.

faq.q: Can I switch from Campaign Monitor to ActiveCampaign?
faq.a: Yes, most email platforms allow you to export subscriber lists and campaign data. Both ActiveCampaign and Campaign Monitor support standard export formats. Plan the migration carefully, test integrations, and consider notifying subscribers about the transition to maintain engagement during the switch.
